title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple examines a photograph taken on the stairs featuring Marina Gregg, Jason Radcliffe, Arthur Badcock, and Mrs. Badcock. She notes that Marina Gregg has a shocked and stunned expression in the image. Dermot provides notes regarding Mrs. Badcock’s interaction with Marina during the photo session. While accounts from Mrs. Bantling, Jason Radle, and Mr. Badcock vary slightly, they all confirm that Mrs. Badcock was a devoted fan who had once gone to great lengths to obtain an autograph from Marina Gregg years prior, even getting out of bed while suffering from an illness. Miss Marple connects this anecdote to a separate mystery regarding why Mrs. Badcock had destroyed a new dress.

Miss Marple then reflects on a housekeeper at the Loriston house who caused confusion by muddling telephone messages due to poor grammar. She also mentions that she had personally sent a seamstress named Gladys Dixon to Bournemouth with money for a vacation to ensure her safety.

Two days later, Miss Knight informs Miss Marple that Miss Conway, who is described as unstable and possessing a poor memory, has been staying at the Bellgrove Hotel in Lyndhurst. Miss Knight suggests that Miss Conway may be suffering from dementia rather than amnesia. Following this discussion, Miss Marple calls Dr. Hedgcock to request a meeting. When the doctor arrives, Miss Marple asserts her own strength and health, arguing that she does not require a permanent live-in caregiver and only needs someone to visit daily for tidying.
